related_questions.title: Elements of a Cover Letter
list: FIRST PARAGRAPH OR INTRODUCTION: ACTS AS BASIS OR PURPOSE OF THE LETTER.
SECOND PARAGRAPH OR BODY: SUPPORTS YOUR CLAIMS FOR BEING THE RIGHT CANDIDATE FOR THIS POSITION/ORGANIZATION.
THIRD PARAGRAPH OR CLOSING: REITERATES YOUR INTEREST IN THE POSITION/ORGANIZATION.

text_blocks.snippet: A great cover letter acts as your professional introduction and pitch. It should be kept to a single page and organized into three to four key paragraphs: an engaging introduction, a body that highlights your most relevant skills and measurable achievements, and a concluding call to action.
snippet_highlighted_words: It should be kept to a single page and organized into three to four key paragraphs: an engaging introduction, a body that highlights your most relevant skills and measurable achievements, and a concluding call to action

text_blocks.snippet: Writing an effective cover letter involves a few essential steps to grab a hiring manager's attention:

list.snippet: Header and Salutation: Include your contact information. Always address the letter to a specific person (like the hiring manager) rather than a generic greeting.
The Hook: Start by expressing genuine enthusiasm for the company and explaining why you are applying for the specific role.
The Pitch: Instead of just summarizing your resume, focus on one or two key achievements. Use concrete examples to show how your background makes you the best fit for the job.
Call to Action: Wrap up by reiterating your enthusiasm, mentioning the specific value you bring, and requesting an interview.

text_blocks.snippet: If you'd like to get started, tell me:

list.snippet:
What job title
are you applying for?

text_blocks.snippet: I can help draft a custom introduction or brainstorm specific examples to highlight in your letter!
